title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Create isolated Python environment and install all required packages for TTA experiments

steps.step1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch 2.0+ with CUDA support: pip install torch torchvision --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu118
steps.step3: Install scientific computing packages: pip install numpy scipy scikit-learn pandas matplotlib seaborn
steps.step4: Install image processing libraries: pip install Pillow opencv-python kornia
steps.step5: Install tqdm for progress bars: pip install tqdm
steps.step6: Verify GPU availability: python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available())'
steps.step7: Create directory structure: mkdir -p data models results figures logs

title: Download and Prepare Datasets
description: Download CIFAR-10/100-C, ImageNet-C (subset), and CIFAR-10.1 for evaluation

steps.step1: Download CIFAR-10/100 from torchvision.datasets, save to ./data/cifar10 and ./data/cifar100
steps.step2: Download CIFAR-10-C from https://zenodo.org/record/2535967, extract to ./data/cifar10-c
steps.step3: Download CIFAR-100-C from https://zenodo.org/record/3555552, extract to ./data/cifar100-c
steps.step4: Download ImageNet-C (5 corruptions only: gaussian_noise, shot_noise, defocus_blur, pixelate, jpeg_compression) to ./data/imagenet-c
steps.step6: Verify dataset integrity: check file counts (CIFAR-10-C: 10k images × 15 corruptions × 5 severities = 750k images)
steps.step7: Pre-compute source prototypes for CIFAR-10/100 using WideResNet-28, save to ./models/prototypes_cifar10.pt and ./models/prototypes_cifar100.pt
steps.step8: Pre-compute source prototypes for ImageNet using ResNet-50, save to ./models/prototypes_imagenet.pt

title: Implement Simple Baseline - Source Model (No Adaptation)
description: Run source model without any adaptation to establish baseline performance

steps.step1: Load pre-trained WideResNet-28 for CIFAR-10/100 (from torchvision or robustness library)
steps.step2: Load pre-trained ResNet-50 for ImageNet (torchvision pretrained)
steps.step3: Evaluate on CIFAR-10-C all 15 corruptions × 5 severity levels, 5 random seeds [2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026]
steps.step4: Evaluate on CIFAR-100-C all 15 corruptions × 5 severity levels, 5 random seeds (NOTE: If time-constrained, evaluate only severity 5)
steps.step5: Evaluate on ImageNet-C 5 selected corruptions × 5 severity levels, 5 random seeds
steps.step6: Record mean accuracy ± standard error per corruption type and severity
steps.step7: Calculate mCE (mean corruption error) relative to AlexNet baseline (provided by Hendrycks & Dietterich)

title: Implement BN Adapt Baseline
description: Implement batch normalization statistics adaptation (Nado et al., 2020) - simple baseline

steps.step1: Implement BN Adapt: update running mean and variance of BN layers using test batch statistics
steps.step2: Batch size: use 200 for CIFAR, 64 for ImageNet (smaller due to larger images)
steps.step3: Evaluate on CIFAR-10-C all severities, 5 random seeds [2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026]
steps.step4: Evaluate on CIFAR-100-C severity 5 only (if time permits, all severities), 5 random seeds
steps.step5: Evaluate on ImageNet-C 5 corruptions, 5 random seeds

title: Implement MEMO Baseline
description: Implement MEMO (Zhang et al., 2022) - strong single-image baseline with fixed augmentations

steps.step1: Implement MEMO augmentation set: 64 augmentations from AutoAugment space (as per paper)
steps.step2: Implement marginal entropy minimization: create virtual batch via augmentation, minimize prediction entropy
steps.step3: Adapt BN parameters with learning rate 1e-3 for CIFAR, 1e-4 for ImageNet
steps.step4: Number of adaptation steps: 1 step per test image (single-image setting)
steps.step5: Evaluate on CIFAR-10-C all severities, 5 random seeds [2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026]
steps.step6: Evaluate on CIFAR-100-C severity 5 only (if time permits, all severities), 5 random seeds
steps.step7: Evaluate on ImageNet-C 5 corruptions, 5 random seeds
steps.step8: Record inference time per image (average over 1000 images)

title: Implement TENT Baseline
description: Implement TENT (Wang et al., 2021) - entropy minimization baseline for comparison

steps.step1: Implement TENT: minimize prediction entropy by updating BN statistics and affine parameters
steps.step2: Use batch size 200 for CIFAR, 64 for ImageNet
steps.step3: Learning rate: 1e-3 for CIFAR, 1e-4 for ImageNet
steps.step4: Evaluate on CIFAR-10-C all severities, 5 random seeds [2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026]
steps.step5: Evaluate on CIFAR-100-C severity 5 only, 5 random seeds

title: Implement Heuristic-APN Baseline
description: Implement simple distance-based severity selection baseline to justify Meta-APN complexity (Feedback #2)

steps.step1: Implement Heuristic-APN: no learned parameters, uses prototype distance heuristic
steps.step2: Compute prototype distance d = min_c ||z - prototype_c|| for test image
steps.step3: Set augmentation severity = max_severity × (1 - exp(-λ × d)) with λ = 2.0
steps.step4: Use fixed set of 8 augmentations (random selection from {gaussian_noise, shot_noise, brightness, contrast, blur, jpeg})
steps.step5: Apply prototype consistency loss (same as APAC-TTA but without learned policy)
steps.step6: Evaluate on CIFAR-10-C all severities, 5 random seeds [2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026]
steps.step7: Evaluate on CIFAR-100-C severity 5 only, 5 random seeds

title: Train Meta-Augmentation Policy Network (Meta-APN)
description: Train lightweight meta-network to predict augmentation policies based on prototype similarity

steps.step1: Define Meta-APN architecture: 2-layer MLP with input dim = feature_dim + num_classes, hidden dim = 64, output dim = num_operations + 2 (severity scale + count)
steps.step3: Training data: Use CIFAR-10/100 training set, split 90/10 for train/val
steps.step4: Augmentation operations (K=8): gaussian_noise, shot_noise, brightness, contrast, blur, jpeg, pixelate, saturate
steps.step5: Gumbel-Softmax implementation: temperature τ annealed from 1.0 to 0.5 over training
steps.step6: Proxy objective: minimize cross-entropy loss on augmented images (differentiable surrogate for accuracy)
steps.step7: Training hyperparameters: batch size 128, learning rate 1e-3 with Adam, 50 epochs
steps.step9: REINFORCE fallback: if Gumbel-Softmax loss > 5.0 after 10 epochs, switch to REINFORCE with baseline
steps.step11: Validate: check that policy varies with prototype distance (correlation test)

title: Implement and Evaluate APAC-TTA (Full Method)
description: Implement complete APAC-TTA with Meta-APN, confidence weighting, and prototype consistency

steps.step3: For each test image: compute prototype distances, get policy from Meta-APN, sample augmentations via Gumbel-Softmax (τ_test = 0.5)
steps.step4: Compute prototype confidence scores: consistency = 1/(1+variance(similarities)) × mean_similarity
steps.step5: Adapt BN parameters using confidence-weighted prototype consistency loss with JS divergence
steps.step6: Temperature for prototype target distribution: T = 0.5
steps.step7: Selective adaptation threshold: μ + 2σ of training prototype distances
steps.step8: Learning rate: 1e-3 for CIFAR, 1e-4 for ImageNet
steps.step9: Evaluate on CIFAR-10-C all 15 corruptions × 5 severities, 5 random seeds [2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026] (Feedback #3)
steps.step10: Evaluate on CIFAR-100-C all 15 corruptions × 5 severities, 5 random seeds (if time-constrained: severity 5 only)
steps.step11: Evaluate on ImageNet-C 5 corruptions × 5 severities, 5 random seeds (expand to full if time permits - Feedback #4)
steps.step12: Record per-image inference time and memory usage

title: Ablation: Fixed Augmentations vs Learned Policy
description: Test contribution of learned augmentation policy by comparing against fixed policy

steps.step1: Implement APAC-TTA-Fixed: use fixed set of 8 augmentations (same as MEMO) instead of Meta-APN
steps.step2: Keep confidence weighting and prototype consistency identical to full method
steps.step3: Evaluate on CIFAR-10-C severity 5 only (most challenging), 5 random seeds
steps.step4: Compare against full APAC-TTA: measure mCE difference
steps.step5: Statistical test: paired t-test on per-image accuracy, p < 0.05 threshold

title: Ablation: Confidence Weighting vs Uniform Weighting
description: Test contribution of confidence-weighted prototype guidance

steps.step1: Implement APAC-TTA-Uniform: use uniform prototype weights instead of confidence-based
steps.step2: Keep Meta-APN and augmentation selection identical to full method
steps.step3: Evaluate on CIFAR-10-C severity 5, 5 random seeds
steps.step4: Compare against full APAC-TTA: measure accuracy difference
steps.step5: Statistical test: paired t-test, p < 0.05 threshold
steps.step6: Analyze which corruptions benefit most from confidence weighting

title: Ablation: Selective Adaptation Threshold
description: Analyze sensitivity to adaptation threshold for out-of-distribution detection

steps.step1: Test 3 threshold values: μ+σ, μ+2σ (default), μ+3σ
steps.step2: Evaluate on CIFAR-10-C severity 5, 5 random seeds
steps.step3: Measure: adaptation rate (% of images adapted), accuracy on adapted images, accuracy on skipped images

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Perform rigorous statistical tests to validate success criteria

steps.step2: For each comparison (APAC-TTA vs MEMO, APAC-TTA vs Heuristic-APN), perform paired t-test
steps.step3: Null hypothesis: methods have equal accuracy; Alternative: APAC-TTA has higher accuracy
steps.step5: Effect size: Cohen's d for practical significance
steps.step6: Report mean ± standard error for all metrics over 5 random seeds (Feedback #3)
steps.step7: Generate summary table with ✓/✗ for each success criterion

title: Natural Distribution Shift Evaluation
description: Evaluate on natural distribution shifts (CIFAR-10.1) to verify no degradation on clean-like data

steps.step1: Evaluate APAC-TTA, MEMO, Source model on CIFAR-10.1, 5 random seeds
steps.step2: Measure: clean accuracy should be within 1% of source model (no degradation on clean-like data)

title: Efficiency Analysis
description: Measure computational overhead of APAC-TTA compared to baselines

steps.step1: Measure per-image inference time: average over 1000 images, exclude first 100 (warmup)
steps.step2: Measure memory usage: peak GPU memory during adaptation
steps.step3: Measure FLOPs: forward pass + adaptation gradient computation
steps.step5: Target: APAC-TTA inference time < 2× MEMO baseline

steps.step1: Figure 1: Accuracy comparison bar chart across methods (Source, BN Adapt, TENT, MEMO, Heuristic-APN, APAC-TTA) on CIFAR-10-C severity 5 with error bars (SE over 5 seeds)
steps.step2: Figure 2: Severity-wise accuracy curves (x-axis: severity 1-5, y-axis: accuracy) for top 3 methods
steps.step3: Figure 3: Per-corruption heatmap showing APAC-TTA improvement over MEMO (positive = APAC-TTA better)
steps.step4: Figure 4: Ablation study bar chart showing contribution of each component (fixed vs learned, uniform vs confidence)
steps.step5: Figure 5: Inference time comparison bar chart
steps.step6: Save all figures to ./figures/ as PDF (vector) and PNG (300 DPI)

steps.step1: Figure 6: Meta-APN policy visualization - show augmentation probability vs prototype distance
steps.step2: Figure 7: Confidence score distribution across prototypes for different corruption types
steps.step3: Table 1: Main results table (CIFAR-10-C, CIFAR-100-C, ImageNet-C) with mCE and accuracy
steps.step4: Table 2: Statistical significance matrix with p-values

title: Contingency Execution (if needed)
description: Fallback experiments if main experiments fail or time permits

steps.step1: If Meta-APN training unstable: Run with REINFORCE instead of Gumbel-Softmax
steps.step2: If time permits (>30 min remaining): Expand ImageNet-C to all 15 corruptions
steps.step3: If Heuristic-APN performs similarly to Meta-APN: Analyze failure cases, report negative result
steps.step4: If confidence weighting degrades: Test alternative confidence metrics (prediction margin, entropy)
steps.step5: Generate contingency report documenting any deviations from plan
